rationale: Ground truth is essentially content-free (LGTM approvals plus a bot verification comment), so neither candidate had real concerns to recall. X's only finding is a valid but trivial nit (missing ge/le Pydantic constraints) that adds little practical value. Y's core validation of the runtime change is equally solid, but it goes further by flagging two specific reliability-suite test files that still encode the old lower bound of 3 in a form (tuple-based assertion) that X's own literal-string sweep would not catch — a plausible, well-grounded, and highly actionable gap with exact file/line and suggested fixes. Y's findings are more substantive and useful even though unverifiable against the truncated diff, giving it the edge in precision-weighted value and actionability.
